days.title: Arrival in Tokyo
days.subtitle: Exploring the buzz of Shinjuku

timeline.title: Arrive at Narita Airport
timeline.detail: After your flight, clear customs and immigration. Follow the signs to JR East Narita Express for a direct train to Shinjuku.

timeline.title: Check in to Hotel Gracery Shinjuku
timeline.detail: Check into your hotel situated in the heart of Shinjuku. It's renowned for the giant Godzilla head on the terrace, offering unique photo-ops.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel Gracery Shinjuku, Shinjuku, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Explore Omoide Yokocho
timeline.detail: Wander through this alleyway filled with tiny izakayas and street food stalls. Try yakitori and local beers for an authentic Japanese experience.

timeline.title: Dinner at Ichiran Ramen
timeline.detail: Savor the famous tonkotsu ramen in a semi-private booth. It's a must-visit for ramen lovers. Order from the menu and customize your bowl.

timeline.title: Visit Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building
timeline.detail: Enjoy panoramic views of Tokyo from the observatories on the 45th floor, free of charge. The city lights are breathtaking at night.
timeline.mapQuery: Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building, Shinjuku, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Nightcap at Golden Gai
timeline.detail: Head to Golden Gai, a famous nightlife district with tiny bars. Each bar has its own unique theme, drink a cocktail and mingle with locals.

localTips: Purchase a Suica or Pasmo card for easy travel on trains and buses around the city.
Some izakayas and bars might not accept credit cards, so keep cash handy.
For a unique experience, try reserving a spot at a themed café in Akihabara.

days.title: Full Day in Tokyo
days.subtitle: Culinary delights and city treasures

timeline.title: Breakfast at Tsukiji Outer Market
timeline.detail: Start your day with fresh sushi and seafood from local vendors in this iconic market. You can find various breakfast options to try.

timeline.title: Visit Senso-ji Temple
timeline.detail: Explore Tokyo's oldest temple, located in Asakusa. Enjoy the shopping street leading up to the temple filled with traditional snacks and souvenirs.

timeline.title: Lunch at Asakusa Menchi
timeline.detail: Try their famous Menchi Katsu, a deep-fried ground meat patty. It's a favorite among locals and very filling, perfect for lunch.

timeline.title: Stroll through Ueno Park
timeline.detail: Make your way to Ueno Park, home to museums and beautiful cherry blossoms. Spend some time enjoying the natural beauty and tranquility.

timeline.title: Dinner at Din Tai Fung
timeline.detail: Savor gourmet Taiwanese dumplings that are extremely popular. Be sure to order their soup dumplings for a tantalizing experience.
timeline.mapQuery: Din Tai Fung, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Explore Harajuku & Takeshita Street
timeline.detail: Experience the youth culture in Harajuku. Try various Japanese treats like crepes and cotton candy on this vibrant shopping street.

highlights: Dumpling feast at Din Tai Fung
localTips: Get to Tsukiji early to avoid crowds and enjoy the freshest seafood.
Try to visit during cherry blossom season for stunning views in Ueno Park.
In Harajuku, consider trying a drink from a themed café.

days.title: Last Day in Tokyo
days.subtitle: Final culinary adventures

timeline.title: Breakfast in Shinjuku
timeline.detail: Enjoy a traditional Japanese breakfast set at a local café. Experience miso soup, grilled fish, and rice for a wholesome start to your day.

timeline.title: Visit Meiji Shrine
timeline.detail: Explore this serene shrine dedicated to the spirits of Emperor Meiji and his wife. The beautiful forested grounds are perfect for a quiet stroll.

timeline.title: Lunch at Narisawa
timeline.detail: Indulge in a top-tier dining experience with seasonal ingredients. This restaurant has a strong focus on sustainability and a unique tasting menu.

timeline.title: Shopping at Ginza
timeline.detail: Wander through the luxury shopping district of Ginza. Explore high-end shops, department stores, and unique boutiques offering local goods.

timeline.title: Farewell Dinner at Sukiyabashi Jiro
timeline.detail: Experience a world-famous sushi meal at Jiro's, known for its meticulous preparation and high-quality fish. Reservations are a must!

timeline.title: Return to Hotel and Prepare for Departure
timeline.detail: Head back to your hotel to pack and unwind. Prepare for your trip back while reminiscing about the amazing culinary adventures you had.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel Gracery Shinjuku, Shinjuku, Tokyo, Japan

localTips: Make dinner reservations at Sukiyabashi Jiro well in advance to secure a spot.
Be sure to sample Tokyo’s local sweet treats like mochi and dorayaki before you leave.
Use your Suica card to pay conveniently at most shops and restaurants.

hotels.name: Hotel Gracery Shinjuku
hotels.note: Famous for its Godzilla head and proximity to Shinjuku station.

budget.note: Round trip from Singapore to Tokyo.
Total for 2 nights at Hotel Gracery Shinjuku.
Budget for meals from street food to fine dining.
Estimate for trains and metro travel during your stay.
Museum and temple entry fees during the trip.

urgent.label: Book Narisawa dinner
urgent.note: Highly recommended restaurant, book in advance.
